title: Cymopterus globosus
taxonomy: Cymopterus globosus is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Cymopterus, which includes several other species of umbelliferous plants. The genus Cymopterus is part of the tribe Selineae, a group of plants that are native to North America. The family Apiaceae, to which Cymopterus globosus belongs, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes carrots, parsley, and other familiar vegetables and herbs. The classification of Cymopterus globosus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Apiales, family Apiaceae, genus Cymopterus, species C. globosus.
uses: Cymopterus globosus is a plant with various medicinal uses. The plant's roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat pain and swelling. Additionally, the plant is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. intro: Cymopterus globosus, also known as globed desert parsley, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae).
